summary: R5 fixes are implemented. Direct-control postwar arrangements now leave a durable, auditable M3 postwar marker through a condition-based defensive-garrison obligation keyed by district. The existing-arrangement guard checks that marker, so direct-control-first arrangements reject later same-district postwar commands instead of allowing implicit replacement or stacking.

tests_run.result: R5 red pass before implementation: direct-control had no durable marker and direct-control-first repeat/replacement commands were accepted.

Worktree includes task-scoped implementation/test/task changes plus task-scoped orchestration message/route files. No forbidden-path changes.

decisions: R5 direct-control marker: direct-control postwar arrangements now create a defensive-garrison obligation with a condition requirement, not a resource amount. This avoids direct resource creation while giving authoritative state a durable marker.

The direct-control marker sourceId is district-keyed as m3.postwar.district.<districtId>.direct-control.<settlementId>, so the guard can detect an applied direct-control postwar arrangement without trusting payload.localPolityId.

hasExistingPostwarGovernanceArrangement still rejects non-direct/non-controller administrative markers from restored-vassal and tribute-only arrangements, and now also rejects district-keyed direct-control marker obligations.
No replacement semantics were added. Repeat same-district postwar commands reject until a future explicitly designed replacement/retirement command exists.

Regression coverage now includes direct-control followed by same-victor direct-control, same-victor tribute-only, same-victor restore-vassal-ruler, and different-victor direct-control. All assert rejection and unchanged state.
risks: R3 public protocol surface remains changed from the original M3 implementation and still needs systems_architect review for long-term command/query naming and compatibility.

The direct-control marker uses existing M3 obligation state as an auditable discriminator. If future design wants a first-class postwar arrangement table, this marker can be migrated or replaced with explicit schema under architect review.
Projection coefficients remain prototype constants for M3 validation and are not final balance.
requested_action: Review the R5 direct-control postwar marker and direct-control-first rejection regressions, then route to QA if systems review accepts.
